articles.title: Pope Leo XIV Makes Historic Apology For Vatican's Role In Legitimizing Slavery
articles.content:
梵蒂冈城（美联社）——教皇利奥十四世周一发表了历史性的道歉，为圣座在奴役合法化方面的作用以及数百年来未能谴责奴役行为道歉，称梵蒂冈的记录是“基督教记忆中的一道伤痕”。

过去的教皇曾为基督徒参与跨大西洋奴隶贸易道歉。但没有一位教皇公开承认，更不用说为过去的教皇在给予欧洲君主明确权威以征服和奴役“异教徒”方面所发挥的作用道歉。

这位历史上首位美国出生的教皇，其家族史既包括被奴役的人，也包括奴隶主。他在周一发布的首份通谕《Magnifica Humanitas》（崇高的尊严）中发表了这一道歉。这份纲领性文件的主题是，在人工智能日益依赖的时代保护人类。利奥将奴隶贸易与他所称的数字革命正在推动的新形式奴役和殖民主义联系起来。

美国黑人天主教徒、活动人士和学者长期以来一直呼吁圣座为在殖民时代人口贸易中的作用赎罪，而不仅仅是对个别基督徒参与的泛泛道歉。

利奥写道：“当我们反思如此众多的人所承受的巨大苦难和羞辱，与他们作为被主无限爱着的每个人的不可估量的尊严形成鲜明对比时，不可能不感到深深的悲痛。为此，我代表教会，真诚地请求宽恕。”

戴顿大学历史学家、《颠覆性习惯》（Subversive Habits）一书的作者香农·迪·威廉姆斯对这一道歉表示欢迎，称这是“迈向许多天主教徒祈祷和努力见证的真正必要真相讲述和赔偿的里程碑式一步”。

威廉姆斯说：“天主教会在白人至上主义的历史中从来不是无辜的旁观者。”她说：“黑人天主教徒等待了很久，才听到梵蒂冈诚实地说出教会在跨大西洋奴隶贸易和畜奴制中的主导作用——从而延伸到当今世界持续的反对黑人种族主义制度。”

几个世纪以来为欧洲殖民者合法化奴役

梵蒂冈一直坚称，它始终维护所有人作为上帝孩子的尊严。但15世纪一系列来自梵蒂冈的指令授权葡萄牙君主征服非洲和美洲，奴役非基督徒。

例如，1452年，教皇尼古拉五世发布了《Dum Diversas》教宗诏书，授予葡萄牙国王及其继承者“入侵、征服、战斗和征服”并夺取任何地方的“撒拉逊人、异教徒和其他不信基督教之名者的敌人”的所有财产——包括土地——的权利。

该诏书还授予葡萄牙人将这些人“沦为永久奴役”的许可。

该诏书和三年后发布的另一份诏书《Romanus Pontifex》构成了“发现论”的基础，这一理论使殖民时代在非洲和美洲的占领土地合法化。

据耶稣会神父、《所有压迫都将停止：天主教会的奴隶制、废奴主义和历史》一书的作者克里斯托弗·J·凯勒曼神父说，尼古拉五世对葡萄牙人的许可在1456年得到教皇卡利克斯特斯三世、1481年得到教皇西克斯图斯四世、1514年得到教皇利奥十世的确认或更新。

西班牙国王获得了美洲的权利。

2023年，圣座正式否定“发现论”，但从未正式废除、撤销或拒绝这些诏书本身。圣座坚持认为，1537年的后续诏书《Sublimis Deus》重申了美洲原住民不应被剥夺自由或财产所有权，不应被奴役。

圣座迟迟未谴责奴役

利奥在通谕中回忆说，他的同名教皇利奥十三世是1888年首位明确谴责奴役的教皇，远在许多国家废除奴役之后。凯勒曼说，在此之前，在古代和中世纪，教会机构甚至教皇——格列高利大帝——都拥有奴隶。

利奥在通谕中承认15世纪的教宗诏书时写道：“早在近代早期，罗马宗座应君主的要求，多次干预，以规范和合法化各种形式的征服，在某些情况下，包括对'异教徒'的奴役。”

利奥说，用今天的标准来评判这些决定的道德性是不可能的。

“然而，我们也不能否认或减少社会和教会在谴责奴役祸害方面的迟缓，”他说。

教皇说，教会长期以来一直肯定每个人作为其教义基础的尊严，“即使花了十八个世纪才明确认识到其与奴役的完全不相容。”

“这构成了基督教记忆中的一道伤痕，一道我们不能认为自己与之无关的伤痕，”他说。

利奥说，如果我们要避免将来再次因未能尊重我们信仰所要求的人类尊严宝库而需要请求宽恕，教会必须坚决谴责与数字技术革命相关的所有形式的人口贩卖。

牛津大学科赫历史中心高级研究员安西娅·巴特勒说，如果利奥想要可信地“谈论当前的技术奴役问题”，他需要承认和弥补教会在历史上的奴役中的共谋。

黑人巴特勒说：“对于被奴役者的后代来说，这再次是教皇非常需要的道歉。”

利奥本人的家族史和过去的道歉

学者凯勒曼对利奥的道歉表示欢迎，但表示需要做更多工作来进一步承认天主教会如何合法化和扩大奴役。

他告诉美联社：“教皇利奥通过今天的承认和道歉，加强了教会的道德信誉。希望未来的文件将更详细地解释教会与蓄奴的关系。作为学者，我对措辞有一些异议，但这是真正非凡的时刻。”

1985年访问喀麦隆期间，圣若望保禄二世代表参与的基督徒向非洲人请求宽恕奴隶贸易，但不是代表教皇。1992年访问塞内加尔戈雷岛——西非最大的奴隶贸易中心——时，他谴责了奴役的不公正，称其为“自称为基督文明的悲剧”。

根据亨利·路易斯·盖茨 Jr.发表的族谱研究，利奥的17位美国祖先是黑人，在人口普查记录中列为黑白混血、黑人、克里奥尔人或自由有色人种。盖茨在《纽约时报》上写道，他的家谱包括奴隶主和被奴役的人。

上个月访问安哥拉期间，利奥在葡萄牙殖民统治时期非洲奴隶贸易重要中心的一个天主教圣地祈祷。在姆沙米玛圣母朝圣地，利奥回忆起安哥拉人几个世纪以来承受的“悲伤和巨大苦难”，但他没有具体提及奴役。

温菲尔德于康涅狄格州米德尔敦报道。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Leo XIV made a historic apology on Monday for the Holy See’s role in legitimizing slavery and for having failed to condemn it for centuries, calling the Vatican’s record a “wound in Christian memory.”

Past popes have apologized for Christians’ involvement in the trans-Atlantic slave trade. But no pope had ever publicly acknowledged, much less apologized for, the role that past popes played in giving European sovereigns explicit authority to subjugate and enslave “infidels.”

Advertisement

History’s first U.S.-born pope, whose family history includes both enslaved people and slave owners, delivered the apology in his first encyclical, “Magnifica Humanitas,” (Magnificent Humanity), which was released Monday.

The sweeping manifesto is about safeguarding humanity in an era of increasing reliance on artificial intelligence. Leo raised the slave trade in relation to what he called the new forms of slavery and colonialism that the digital revolution is fueling.

Black American Catholics, activists and scholars have long called for the Holy See to atone for its role in the colonial-era trade in human beings, beyond generic apologies for the involvement of individual Christians.

Advertisement

“It is impossible not to feel deep sorrow when contemplating the immense suffering and humiliation endured by so many in stark contrast to their immeasurable dignity as persons infinitely loved by the Lord,” Leo wrote. “For this, in the name of the church, I sincerely ask for pardon.”

Shannen Dee Williams, historian at the University of Dayton and author of the 2022 history of American Black Catholic nuns, “Subversive Habits,” welcomed the apology as a “monumental step toward the kind of essential truth-telling and reparation that many Catholics have prayed and worked to witness.”

“The Catholic Church has never been an innocent bystander in the history of white supremacy,” said Williams. “Black Catholics have waited a long time to hear the Vatican speak honestly about the church’s leading roles in the trans-Atlantic slave trade and chattel slavery―and thus by extension the enduring systems of anti-Black racism in the world today.”

Advertisement

Centuries of legitimizing slavery for European colonizers

The Vatican has insisted that it always upheld the dignity of all human beings as children of God. But a series of 15th-century directives from the Vatican authorized Portuguese sovereigns to conquer Africa and the Americas and enslave non-Christians.

In 1452, for example, Pope Nicholas V issued the papal bull Dum Diversas, which gave the Portuguese king and his successors the right “to invade, conquer, fight and subjugate” and take all possessions — including land — of “Saracens, and pagans, and other infidels, and enemies of the name of Christ” anywhere.

Advertisement

The bull also gave the Portuguese permission “to reduce their persons to perpetual slavery.”

That bull and another issued three years later, Romanus Pontifex, formed the basis of the Doctrine of Discovery, the theory that legitimized the colonial-era seizure of land in Africa and the Americas.

Nicholas V’s permissions to the Portuguese were confirmed or renewed by Pope Callixtus III in 1456, Pope Sixtus IV in 1481 and Pope Leo X in 1514, according to the Rev. Christopher J. Kellerman, a Jesuit priest and author of “All Oppression Shall Cease: A History of Slavery, Abolitionism, and the Catholic Church.”

Spanish kings received the rights for the Americas.

In 2023, the Vatican formally repudiated the Doctrine of Discovery, but it never formally rescinded, abrogated or rejected the bulls themselves. The Vatican insists that a later bull, Sublimis Deus in 1537, reaffirmed that Indigenous peoples shouldn’t be deprived of their liberty or the possession of their property, and weren’t to be enslaved.

Advertisement

Holy See late to condemn slavery, Leo says

In his encyclical, Leo recalled that his namesake, Pope Leo XIII, was the first pope to explicitly condemn slavery in 1888, long after many countries had abolished it. Before that, in antiquity and the Middle Ages, church institutions and even popes — Gregory the Great — had slaves, Kellerman said.

In acknowledging the 15th century papal bulls, Leo wrote in his encyclical: “Already in the early modern period, the Apostolic See of Rome, responding to the requests of sovereigns, intervened several times in order to regulate and legitimize forms of subjugation, and, in certain cases, including the enslavement of ‘infidels.’”

Leo said it wasn’t possible to judge the morality of the decisions with today’s standards.

“Yet neither can we deny or diminish the delay with which both society and the church came to denounce the scourge of slavery,” he said.

Advertisement

The pope said that the church has long affirmed the dignity of every human being as the basis of its doctrine, “even if it took eighteen centuries for its full incompatibility with slavery to be explicitly recognized.”

“This constitutes a wound in Christian memory, one from which we cannot consider ourselves detached,” he said.

Leo said that the church must firmly condemn all forms of trafficking related to the digital technological revolution “if we want to avoid the need to ask for pardon again in the future for having failed to respect the treasure of human dignity that is required by our faith.”

Advertisement

Anthea Butler, senior fellow at the Koch History Center, Oxford University, said Leo needed to acknowledge and atone for the church’s complicity in historic slavery if he wanted to credibly “speak to the current issues of technological enslavement.”

“For descendants of enslaved persons, this is once again a much needed apology from the pope,” said Butler, who is Black.

Leo’s own family history and past apologies

Kellerman, the scholar, welcomed Leo’s apology but said more needs to be done to further acknowledge how the Catholic Church legitimized and expanded slavery.

Advertisement

“Pope Leo has strengthened the moral credibility of the church with this admission and apology today,” he told The Associated Press. “Hopefully a future document will explain in more detail the church’s involvement with slaveholding. As a scholar I have some quibbles with the wording, but this is a truly remarkable moment.”

During a 1985 visit to Cameroon, St. John Paul II asked forgiveness of Africans for the slave trade on behalf of Christians who participated in it, but not the popes. In a 1992 visit to Goree Island, Senegal, which was the largest slave-trading center in West Africa, he denounced the injustice of slavery and called it a “tragedy of a civilization that called itself Christian.”

According to genealogical research published by Henry Louis Gates Jr., 17 of Leo’s American ancestors were Black, listed in census records as mulatto, Black, Creole or a free person of color. His family tree includes slaveholders and enslaved people, Gates wrote in The New York Times.

Advertisement

During a visit to Angola last month, Leo prayed at a Catholic shrine at the site of an important hub of the African slave trade during Portugal’s colonial rule. While at the Sanctuary of Mama Muxima, Leo recalled the “sorrow and great suffering” Angolans endured for centuries, but he didn’t refer specifically to slavery.

___

Winfield reported from Middletown, Connecticut.

Related

vatican

slavery

pope leo xiv

Pope Leo Issues Blunt Message For Profit-Driven Polluters

articles.title: What We Know And Don't Know About The Emerging Deal To End The Iran War
articles.content:
开罗（美联社）——美国和伊朗似乎正接近达成协议，以结束战争并重新开放霍尔木兹海峡。美国总统唐纳德·特朗普周一表示，谈判“进展顺利”，同时重申如果无法达成协议，冲突将会继续。

目前尚不清楚协议将如何以及何时最终敲定，以及其各项条款何时生效。消息来自两位地区官员和一位美国官员，他们要求匿名以讨论敏感谈判内容。

由伊朗议会主席穆罕默德·巴盖尔·加利布阿夫率领的伊朗代表团周一前往卡塔尔进行谈判，但尚不清楚将讨论什么内容。加利布阿夫上月在巴基斯坦与美国副总统JD·万斯举行了历史性的面对面会谈。

伊朗外交部发言人伊斯梅尔·巴盖伊表示，尽管“大部分问题已达成共识”，但仍有工作要做。“说这意味着协议即将签署是任何人都不能声称的，”他告诉媒体。

**已知情况如下：**

**战争将结束**

自美国和以色列发动战争以来的12周内，已有伊朗最高领袖阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊和其他高级官员被杀，德黑兰坚持任何协议都必须集中于结束所有战线的战斗。这包括黎巴嫩，伊朗支持的真主党武装组织在战争开始两天后就在那里与以色列作战。

自4月7日以来，尽管偶尔有无人机和导弹袭击阿联酋和霍尔木兹海峡的一些交火，脆弱的停火协议一直有效。更持久的停火将允许全球航运，包括估计占世界石油20%的运输，重新通过海峡。这也将允许该地区能源和其他基础设施的重建。

两位地区官员表示，协议草案包括结束以色列与真主党之间的战争，以及承诺不干涉该地区国家的内政。这指的是伊朗对其代理人的支持，包括也门胡塞武装、加沙的哈马斯武装分子和伊拉克的什叶派武装组织。

美国希望以色列能自由应对他们认为在黎巴嫩的威胁，而伊朗拒绝这一点，一位地区官员表示。美国官员表示，协议将保证以色列在自卫中应对迫在眉睫的威胁的权利。

**霍尔木兹海峡将逐步重新开放**

美国和以色列的公开目标是拆除伊朗的核计划、限制其导弹项目并结束其对武装代理人的支持。

但在美国和以色列发动战争后，伊朗对霍尔木兹海峡的封锁迅速成为全球关注的焦点，因为数百艘载有石油、天然气、化肥和其他物资的船只被困，导致全球价格飙升。

根据新兴协议，海峡将逐步重新开放，与美国在4月17日对伊朗港口实施封锁的同时进行。地区官员表示，封锁限制了伊朗出口石油和获得急需资金的能力。

一位了解谈判内容的官员表示，美国将允许伊朗通过制裁豁免销售其石油。制裁缓解和解冻数十亿美元的伊朗资金将在60天内谈判。

**伊朗将放弃其高浓缩铀库存**

伊朗的核计划和国际社会对其可能追求核武器的担忧是紧张局势的根源。美国和以色列曾考虑进行复杂的军事行动，以清除其高浓缩铀。

根据潜在协议，德黑兰将同意放弃其库存，地区官员表示。一位直接了解谈判内容的官员表示，伊朗如何放弃库存将在60天内进一步谈判。一些库存可能被稀释，其余转移到第三国，俄罗斯已提出接收。

美国官员确认了60天的期限，并表示如果伊朗不放弃其库存，将没有制裁缓解。

巴盖伊，伊朗发言人表示：“谈判的重点是结束战争，目前我们不讨论核问题的细节。”

伊朗拥有440.9公斤（972磅）浓缩至60%纯度的铀，距离武器级90%的水平只有一步之遥，国际原子能机构表示。

伊朗表示其拥有“不可剥夺”的核技术权利，同时坚持其计划是和平的。周日，总统马苏德·佩泽希安告诉国营电视台，他们已准备好“向世界保证我们不追求核武器”。

**似乎缺失的内容**

“与伊朗伊斯兰共和国的谈判进展顺利！”特朗普周一在社交媒体上写道。“要么对所有人都是一笔大交易，要么就没有协议——回到前线和射击，但比以往更大更强大——没有人想要那样！”

尽管有进展的声明，但包括伊朗是否能浓缩铀以及到什么水平，以及其导弹计划的命运，特别是以色列认为这是一个主要威胁，这些问题似乎仍未解决。

至于伊朗在谈判期间的过去目标，似乎没有提及任何美国军队从该地区撤出，或战争造成的损失的赔偿。

___
苏维尔瓦报告于华盛顿，安娜来自纽约州洛维尔。

&

CAIRO (AP) — The United States and Iran appear to be closing in on a deal to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. President Donald Trump said Monday that negotiations are “proceeding nicely,” while reiterating his warning that fighting would resume if no deal is reached.

It is not clear when or how the deal might be finalized and when its various parts will take effect. Details come from two regional officials and a U.S. official who spoke on condition of anonymity to discuss the sensitive negotiations.

Advertisement

An Iranian delegation led by parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf traveled to Qatar on Monday as part of talks, though it wasn’t immediately clear what would be discussed. Qalibaf led historic face-to-face talks with Vice President JD Vance in Pakistan last month.

Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesman, Esmail Baghaei, said that while understandings have been reached on “a large portion of the issues,” there was still work to be done. “To say that this means an agreement is on the verge of being signed is not something anyone can claim,” he told reporters.

Here’s what we know and don’t know:

The War Would End

In the 12 weeks since the U.S. and Israel launched the war with attacks that killed Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and other top officials, Tehran has insisted that any deal focus on ending the fighting on all fronts. That includes Lebanon, where the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militant group has been fighting Israel since two days into the war.

Advertisement

A fragile ceasefire has held since April 7, despite occasional drone and missile attacks on the United Arab Emirates and some exchanges of fire in the Strait of Hormuz. A more permanent truce would allow for global shipping, including an estimated 20% of the world’s oil, to begin flowing through the strait again. It also would allow the rebuilding of energy and other infrastructure in the region.

Both regional officials said the draft deal includes an end to the war between Israel and Hezbollah, as well as a commitment to not interfere in the domestic affairs of countries in the region. That’s a reference to Iran’s support for proxies, including Houthi rebels in Yemen, Hamas militants in Gaza and Shiite armed groups in Iraq.

The U.S. wants Israel to have a free hand to respond to what they view as threats in Lebanon, while Iran rejects it, one regional official said. The U.S. official said the deal would guarantee Israel’s right to act against imminent threats in self-defense.

Advertisement

The Strait Of Hormuz Would Reopen Gradually

The stated objectives of the U.S. and Israel were to dismantle Iran’s nuclear program, curb its missile program and end its support for armed proxies.

But Iran’s chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz, which was open to international shipping before the war, quickly shot to the top of global concerns as hundreds of ships carrying oil, natural gas, fertilizer and other supplies were stranded, causing a spike in prices far beyond the region.

Under the emerging agreement, the strait would gradually reopen in parallel with the U.S. ending the blockade of Iran’s ports it imposed on April 17, the regional officials said. The blockade has limited Iran’s ability to ship its oil and bring in badly needed cash for its long-suffering economy.

Advertisement

The U.S. would allow Iran to sell its oil through sanctions waivers, said one of the officials, who has been briefed on the negotiations. Sanctions relief and the release of billions of dollars in frozen Iranian funds would be negotiated during a 60-day period, the official said.

Iran Would Give Up Its Stockpile Of Highly Enriched Uranium

Iran’s nuclear program and international concerns over its possible pursuit of a nuclear weapon underlie the tensions. The U.S. and Israel have considered highly complex military operations to go in and take out its highly enriched uranium.

Under the potential deal, Tehran would agree to give up that stockpile, according to the regional officials. One official, with direct knowledge of the negotiations, said how Iran would give it up would be subject to further talks over the 60-day period. Some would likely be diluted and the rest transferred to a third country, the official said. Russia has offered to take it.

Advertisement

A U.S. official confirmed the 60-day period and said if Iran doesn’t give up its stockpile, there will be no sanctions relief.

Baghaei, the Iranian spokesperson, said “the focus of the negotiations is on ending the war, and at this stage we are not discussing the details of the nuclear issue.”

Iran has 440.9 kilograms (972 pounds) of uranium that is enriched up to 60% purity, a short, technical step from weapons-grade levels of 90%, according to the International Atomic Energy Agency.

Advertisement

Iran says it has an “inalienable” right to nuclear technology while insisting its program is peaceful. On Sunday, President Masoud Pezeshkian told state TV they were ready “to assure the world that we are not after a nuclear weapon.”

What Appears To Be Missing

“Negotiations with the Islamic Republic of Iran are proceeding nicely!” Trump wrote on social media Monday. “It will only be a Great Deal for all or, no Deal at all — Back to the Battlefront and shooting, but bigger and stronger than ever before — And nobody wants that!”

Despite the claims of progress, several issues appear to be unsettled, including whether Iran would be able to enrich uranium and to what level, and the fate of its missile program, which Israel in particular views as a major threat.

Advertisement

While the United States and Israel entered the war with the stated hope of seeing Iranians rise up against their government after nationwide protests early in the year, any discussion of leadership change in Tehran appears to be out.

As for Iran’s past stated aims during negotiations, there appears to be no mention of any withdrawal of U.S. forces from the region, or for reparations for the damage the war has caused.

___

Superville reported from Washington and Anna from Lowville, New York.

articles.title: U.S. Military Strikes Iranian Boats, Missile Launch Sites: CENTCOM
articles.content:
华盛顿5月25日（路透社）——美军周一在伊朗南部发动打击，目标是试图布设水雷的船只和导弹发射阵地，美方称这些是防御行动。

美国中央司令部在声明中表示，打击旨在"保护部队免受伊朗军队威胁"。

司令部发言人、海军上校蒂姆·霍金斯说："中央司令部在持续停火期间继续保卫部队，同时保持克制。"

（报道：菲尔·斯图尔特；编辑：索尼娅·保罗）
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

WASHINGTON, May 25 (Reuters) - The U.S. military carried out strikes on Monday in southern Iran against targets including boats attempting to lay mines and missile launch sites, in what it described as defensive actions.

U.S. Central Command said in a statement the strikes were designed “to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces.”

Advertisement

“U.S. Central Command continues to defend our forces while using restraint during the ongoing ceasefire,” said Navy Captain Tim Hawkins, a Central Command spokesperson.

(Reporting by Phil Stewart; Editing by Sonali Paul)
